yesterdayAnalysis: Full rest day before race — exactly what was prescribed for Friday and correctly executed. The 9.9h sleep on Friday night (score 82) was excellent pre-race recovery, showing the body was primed and ready. Yesterday's rest allowed full glycogen restoration and neuromuscular freshness for today's International distance race.
todayRecommendation: Race hard but smart: push the swim from the gun, stay controlled on the hilly bike (1,112ft over 2 loops — don't spike watts on climbs), then execute a strong run off the bike. This is your final dress rehearsal for Oregon 70.3 pacing discipline in 21 days — treat it as data collection for race execution, not just a time trial.
weekProgress: Week 10 of 13: Currently at 211 TSS of 300 target (70% complete) with 0 days remaining including today. The race itself should add 60-75 TSS, bringing the week to ~275-286 TSS (92-95% of target) — acceptable for a taper week leading into a B-race. All planned sessions completed: Mon easy spin, Tue pool intervals, Wed easy swim, Thu brick, Fri rest, Sat shakeout executed correctly.
keyInsight: Today's race will reveal whether conservative bike pacing translates to strong run execution on a hilly course — critical validation 3 weeks before Oregon 70.3.
